content: How are you? Why did you have to come here?
I bring my son here, so I thought I should see someone myself.
Okay, why did you feel the need to see someone?
Actually, I get very angry. My anger is too much.
What happens when you get angry?
When I get angry, I shout a lot. I won't lie, sometimes I even hit my son. But after the madam told me not to, I try not to do it anymore.
Does anything else happen? How do you know you're getting angry?
I only realize I was angry after I've gotten angry. Then I feel like I shouldn't have done it.
Do you notice any physical changes?
Yes, when I get angry, my ears get hot, my forehead sweats, and I feel restless.
Hmm, has this anger ever caused any harm? For example, at work or anywhere else?
No, not yet. I'm a school teacher, so I have to deal with many children. Sometimes it's annoying, but it's fine.
What about with others at home?
Yes, sometimes it happens with my wife because of my son, but not that much. I want my anger to decrease a bit.
Look, everyone gets angry, you do, I do, but how we express that anger is up to us. We have to try to express anger in a way that doesn't harm ourselves or others.
Yes, that's true.
And it's a good thing that you understand what physical changes occur when you get angry. It's very important to understand that.
Yes, I understand, but I still can't stop myself. After showing anger, I feel like I shouldn't have done that.
Hmm, I can see that this anger issue is very distressing for you.
Yes, madam, it feels very bad.
That's why we have to try to recognize or understand these physical changes so that we can realize we are getting angry.
Okay, I can try that.
Yes, it will take time, practice is needed, and then it will be possible to understand these signs. Has it ever happened that you understood and were able to stop yourself from getting angry?
Yes, sometimes it happens that I realize I'm getting angry, and then I try to calm down.
How do you try to calm yourself?
Nothing specific. I try to do something else.
Okay, instead of getting angry, we can do some other things that might reduce anger and not harm anyone. Is there anything you do that makes you feel good?
Hmm, I like listening to music, I like reading newspapers.

That's very good. So, what can we do first is to move away from the person or place where the anger is happening. Then you can try some things like deep breathing, listening to music, reading newspapers, or walking for 10-20 minutes, or counting from 100 to 0. Whatever feels right to you at that moment. You can also try other things that you think will reduce your anger or make you feel good.
Okay, yes, I can try that.
Hmm, is there anything else you want to say that I haven't asked?
No, nothing else for now, I'll do these first.
Okay, I'll also write these down for you so it's easier. If there's any problem next time, we'll discuss it.
How are you?
I am fine now.
Since when have you been seeing a doctor here?
I have been seeing a doctor here once a month for about 4-5 months now.
When you first came, what kind of problems did you have?
My problem was with breathing and coughing. It was difficult to exhale. It was difficult to inhale.
Okay, how are you now?
I am moderately well now. The difficulty in breathing has reduced a bit, but not completely. But the cough is getting worse in the cold.
Hmm hmm
I understand that my cough is increasing due to the cold.
Okay, are you still addicted to anything?
I used to smoke a lot of bidis and cigarettes. Now I am smoking less. Now it's probably not more than two or three.
Can you tell me how much you used to smoke before?
There was no fixed amount before. Still, it used to be about one to one and a half bundles of bidis.
And how many are in one bundle?
Now they give 18. Earlier, they used to give 25 in one bundle. Now it has reduced to 18.
Oh... so you used to smoke about 25 a day, right? And for how many years have you been addicted?
For a long time, almost 45 years.
And how much has it cost over the years?
Yes, it has cost a lot. Like, 10 rupees and 1 rupee for matches.
Hmm, so it used to cost about 300 rupees a month, right? Has it ever happened that you completely quit the addiction for a while?
Yes, I quit one thing for a long time. I used to drink a lot of alcohol before. Then I quit for some days, then I drank again for some days. Now it's completely stopped, and I don't drink anymore.
How many years has it been since you completely stopped drinking alcohol?
It hasn't been that long since it completely stopped. It's been about 6 months. Sometimes I used to drink with friends, those of my age. I drank in moderation.
You said in moderation. How much was that?
I can't say for sure.
Okay, you said you smoke much less bidis than before, but how many are you still smoking?
I am smoking two to three bidis.
Is the desire to smoke the same as before?
I don't have a strong desire. I haven't smoked since morning. But I need one bidi in the morning. I have some toilet problems, that's why I need one. After that, I don't feel like smoking anymore.
Have you been taking the nicotine gum that was recommended here?
No, I don't take it anymore, I have stopped.
Okay, what's the reason for stopping?
It didn't feel good. It left a bad smell in my mouth, that's why.
Oh, okay. If you have a problem, you should talk about it. But it does help you reduce the desire to smoke, doesn't it?
Hmm, it did.
What do you think is the reason that you used to smoke about 25 bidis a day, and now it has come down to 2-3?

When I come to the doctor, they always tell me not to smoke bidis, not to get addicted. I don't just see doctors here, I have seen doctors outside too, and everyone tells me not to get addicted. Everyone tells me to reduce smoking bidis. Back then, I had the physical ability, so I smoked.
Okay, why do you think doctors forbid you so much?
Doctors forbid us for our well-being, but we don't listen.
Do you think this bidi addiction is causing you any problems?

Yes, there are definitely problems. It's related to breathing. The smoke that we inhale goes into our body and gets stuck, and that causes shortness of breath and coughing. These things happen because of that.

Yes, you are right. The smoke from bidis and cigarettes accumulates in our lungs, and it reduces the capacity of our lungs, which causes problems with breathing and increases coughing. And it's very good that you have been able to reduce it from 25 to 3-4. Tell me one more thing, can anything else happen if you get too addicted, besides shortness of breath?
Yes, I have heard that cancer can happen.
So, do you think that you can reduce it even more from 25 to 4?
Yes, everything is in the mind. If you want, you can do it.
But it's a bit difficult for you, isn't it?
Yes, it's very difficult to quit in the morning.
Okay, besides morning, when else do you feel like smoking?
Besides morning, I don't feel like it much.
So, in what situations do you smoke the remaining bidis?
There's no fixed time for the others. Maybe I smoke after two or three hours.
Do you smoke alone or with others?
No, my friends mostly know that I have quit smoking bidis and I am not smoking anymore. They don't tell me much either.
Okay, okay.
I have some good friends in this regard. If they see me, they say, "Didn't you say you quit smoking bidis? Why are you smoking?"
So, when do you feel like smoking the others?
I work in the fields. If I make a mistake, I feel like smoking a bidi. To reduce the tension a bit. When I think about why I made this mistake, I feel like smoking one.
So, it seems that when you are worried about something, smoking a bidi makes you feel a little better.
Yes, I feel like, why did I make this mistake? I feel a bit strange then.
How do you feel then, can you tell me?
I feel a bit bad then, that I am forgetting so much, I didn't use to forget before.
Hmm, how is your mood otherwise?
My mood is good, I don't feel bad much.
So, you only feel bad when you make a mistake, right? How do you feel about working otherwise?
I like working, my mind is focused on work. But I can do less because of breathing difficulties. If I go towards dust, it gets more difficult.
Everyone makes mistakes, right? Still, why do you feel bad?
It didn't use to happen before. Now it does. And I can do less work, that's why.
Your health is still not good, right? And you are also getting older, 60 years old. You can rest a bit now. What do you think?
I can, but I have to work on my own land a bit.

When you feel like smoking a bidi when you are forgetting things, can you do something else? Can you take nicotine gum? I understand that it might give you a different smell in your mouth, but it will help you when you feel like smoking. It's not like you feel like it all day, right?
No, it's not like that.

When learning to ride a bicycle, it takes some time to learn alone, right? You fall repeatedly. But if someone holds you a bit, it becomes much easier. Nicotine gum will do the same thing, it will make it much easier for you to quit smoking. You should think about what is more important, good health or a little smell in your mouth, right?
Yes, that's right.
How are you?
Good
How old are you?
What do you do?
Mason
Who is at home?
Mother, father, my two daughters, my wife, my brother, my brother's daughter and wife.
So you all live together, right?
In the same house but not together.
Okay, why did you have to come here?
My head pain, heavy eyes, all this.
Hmm, you showed it to another department before, right?
Yes, I showed it in medicine there.
Did you start medication from there?
Yes, I'm taking it.
Did it help?
It has reduced a bit after taking these medicines.
Why did you have to come to this department today?
I can't say that, they sent me here.
Do you have any kind of addiction?
I don't have any kind of addiction, I only smoke bidis.
Okay, from what age did the addiction start?
I can't say that.
Still try to remember a little?
How many years would it be. I am 40 years old. I have been smoking since I was roughly 18-20 years old.
Hmm, how many bidis do you smoke a day?
One packet of bidis for day and night.
And one packet means how many?
Around 15-16.
Do you do any other kind of addiction besides bidis?
No.
So how much do you spend on addiction per month?
Whatever I spend in a month, it becomes difficult to run my household.
Okay, I was asking how much you spend only on bidis?
It costs about 300 rupees per month only for bidis.
Has it ever happened that you were able to quit addiction for many days in a month?
I tried for a few days to quit, but I work as a mason, so it's difficult.
Difficult meaning?
When everyone smokes, I feel like it.
How long were you able to quit?
I quit for a few days, not for long.
What was the reason for quitting?
I felt that smoking bidis is harmful. I won't smoke anymore.
Okay, what harm do you think was happening?

Everyone says smoking bidis is harmful, it causes harm. Let's see if I can quit smoking bidis. I tried to quit. Now I work as a mason, and when the laborers and masons all smoke bidis, everyone says "have one", and I feel like it, and that's how it starts again.
Hmm, now I will ask you some questions related to addiction.
Okay.
How old are you now?
My age on paper is 57, but my real age is 62.
Who is at home now?
My daughter stays at home all day, and I am alone at night.
Who did you come with today?
My elder son-in-law and younger daughter.
What do you do all day now?
I don't do anything.
Do you go out? Do you meet people?
No, I stay at home, always at home.
You said you are not working, so how are you running the household?
My daughter runs it. Both daughters pretty much run it.
Okay, why did you first have to come here?

I first came here because I was having trouble breathing in the cold, so I had to use nasal medication. I saw a doctor there, who had many degrees. He told me to do a Holter test, and if it was positive, I would need a pacemaker. But then there's a doctor near our house, I showed him the report, and he said there's nothing, just a minor issue, and he would fix it. But then my daughter said it's better to see a big doctor. So I came here. Here too, the doctor said the same thing, that there's a minor problem. He said the same thing as the other doctor.
You said you are having trouble breathing. How long has this been happening?
Only when it's cold.
For how many years has it been happening?
Two years.
You said you haven't been working for 3-4 years.
3 years. I haven't worked for 3 years, completely unemployed.
Is there a reason for this?
The reason is that after my mother died, my daughter doesn't want me to go out.
Oh... how did your mother die?
Cancer. Cancer from a tumor. She was being treated at RG.
And since then, they don't let you go out, right? What's the reason for this?
Yes, they have completely retired me.
Hmm... hmm... Do you like this?
I don't like staying at home. If my daughter doesn't let me go out, there's nothing I can do.
Do you go to the market/meet friends?
I go to the market, do some shopping. And I cook. I've always cooked alone.
Okay.
And I don't socialize with friends. I've never had friends since childhood. My only friend, she's gone.
I understand you were very close. So, there's a lingering sadness?
Yes, there's a lingering sadness.
How much do you still remember about her?
I do remember. I can't sleep all night. I don't sleep at all at night.
Has it ever happened that you wished you were dead rather than living alone?
No, not at all. My daughters are there; I live for them now.
Do you have any addictions?
Bidi, paan, and tea. Tea usually starts from 3 AM.
Okay, so when do you sleep then?

I stay awake all night. Watching TV, I might get a glimpse of sleep. That's it. I lie in bed for half an hour, then maybe sleep for an hour. I don't get sleep. I haven't been sleeping at all for two years.
Hmm, okay, you said you have a bidi addiction? How many do you have to smoke?
I've reduced bidis now because I'm seeing a doctor. I had one at 3 PM today and haven't had any since.
Okay, from what age did it start?
The bidi started pretty much when I was 25 years old.
You said it has decreased now. How many did you smoke initially?
Did it gradually increase after that?
Then I increased it. Later, it was one bundle, one and a half bundles. One and a half packets.
How many were in one packet?
There were 17. Around 20-22. Then I reduced it, and now I smoke 7-8.
How long has it been since you reduced it?
It's been a week.
And for this week, how many bidis have you been smoking?
For a week, still around 7-8.
You said you eat paan. How much paan do you eat?
I eat a lot of paan. With lime and betel nut.
Since when have you been eating paan?
It must be around 25 years.
How many paans do you eat in a day?
Many. There's no count for paan. My mouth gets sour, and then I eat one.
Still, how many do you generally eat?
Say, 30. Normal paan with lime and betel nut.
How much money do you spend on bidis and paan in total per day?
It used to cost a lot. 300 rupees for paan a month and say 200 rupees for bidis, so 500 rupees.
Hmm.
But not so much now. I don't eat paan anymore.
Paan completely stopped?
No, I eat about 10. Now I tear one paan and eat it in 2-3 sittings. I don't eat it whole.
How long after waking up do you need a bidi? Within 5 minutes? Half an hour/1 hour, or more than that?
I need a bidi right after waking up.
Is it difficult to stop yourself from smoking in places where it's forbidden?
It is difficult, but what can I do, I stop myself.
Which bidi will be the hardest to quit? The one you smoke in the morning or the ones you smoke throughout the day?
It's not difficult to quit the ones throughout the day, but the one I smoke in the morning will be very difficult to quit.
Do you smoke more at the beginning of the day or throughout the day?
More at the beginning.
Has it ever happened that you are so sick that you can't get out of bed, but you still need to smoke a bidi?
No, I haven't been that sick to lie in bed yet.
When you have shortness of breath, do you still smoke a bidi?
No, I don't then.
Do you have to eat paan in the same way? Right after waking up in the morning?
Yes, after a cup of tea, I have to eat a paan. It's not with zarda.
Has it ever happened that you completely quit addiction?
I once didn't smoke bidi for 14 days straight. In 2007. I had an accident and was hospitalized, so it was completely stopped for 14 days.
So, it started again after you returned home, right?
Yes.
Have you ever done any other addiction besides this?
Once during Vishwakarma Puja, I drank alcohol for a day, and then never again. Due to peer pressure.
Do you have problems with blood pressure and sugar?
My blood pressure shows very high here, but it's never that high at home. At home, it shows 100/70. I don't have sugar.
Okay, do you have any major illness?
No.
How is your mood? Do you have any worries?
No, nothing like that.
Is there anyone at home who used to or still has an addiction?
My father used to, but he passed away many years ago.
What kind of addiction did he have?
What other addiction would he have? Besides water, what else would he do? He drank a lot of alcohol.
Does anyone at home have any mental or physical problems?
No, nothing like that.
You used to smoke 20-22 bidis a day, and now you've reduced it to 7-8. Why are you trying to reduce it?
It would be better if I quit bidis completely. It's not stopping entirely.
Why do you want to stop this?
Since I've developed a slight chest problem, I've reduced it.
So, you think there's a connection between your chest problem and bidi addiction?
If the chest problem increases further, that's why I want to quit. I think the chest problem can worsen due to bidis. I also have a cold and cough problem.
Is it possible that bidi addiction worsens your cold and cough?
I think it does. It didn't happen so much before, but now it happens more often.
So, you want to quit because your chest problem is getting worse, right? Have you thought about how you'll quit?
You tell me how to quit. You will surely give some medicine.
Okay, what do your daughters say about this addiction?

My daughters are scolding me. They keep saying I have to quit, I have to quit, it won't work if I don't. I told my daughter then that I can't quit all at once, I'll quit gradually. Now that I'm at 7, I have to reduce it completely. I just have to do morning, noon, night. This way, gradually, I'll quit completely.
Uh-huh.
I can't quit all at once like that; I'll quit gradually. If I quit suddenly, it might backfire.
Backfire, meaning?
My heart might block. Meaning, I'll have a chest pain, a restlessness, that will happen.
Have you ever heard of something like this happening?
No, I haven't heard of anything like that, but I feel some pain in my chest.

That might be because your bidi habit has now become an addiction, right? It's becoming difficult for you to stay without it. You've started to depend on it too much. For example, your wife was with you for so long, you had a habit, but now that she's not there, you're having problems. You can't sleep at night, you have no one to talk to, you're in pain. In the same way, when you're not addicted, due to that habit, you're experiencing pain, which we call withdrawal, and because of that, you then feel like having another bidi or getting addicted.
Yes, when she left, my bidi smoking increased. Before that, one packet would last me all day, but for two years, she's not there, I feel alone, there's no one to forbid me, so it increased.
What do you think was the reason for it increasing then?
Due to tension. What happens when family members are not there. When there's pressure, I feel relieved if I have that, that's all.
You said you feel relieved, does that last for a long time?
No, it's for a moment. Then I have to smoke another bidi.
So, you understand that bidi is not a permanent solution for all worries. And you also understand that if you have more tension, your tendency to smoke bidis also increases, right?
Yes. Lately, even if there's more tension, there's nothing I can do, I have to reduce the bidi. Because the shortness of breath is increasing. But quitting paan is not possible; my mouth gets sour.

So, what is understood from your words is that you want to quit bidi addiction because it is increasing your shortness of breath and cold and cough, and your daughters also don't want you to smoke bidis anymore. There's a gum called nicotine gum that helps reduce our craving for bidis. You chew it once and keep it between your cheek and gum, from which an extract comes out that will reduce your craving. You have to keep it for about 30-45 minutes and then discard it, and it can be taken every 2 hours.
Hello. I am a clinical psychologist, meaning a mental health specialist. Today I will ask you some questions about your addiction. Is that okay?
Yes, please ask.
How old are you now?
Okay. How far have you studied?
Up to graduation.
Did you have any job after that?
I went for jobs, took exams, but didn't get any.
Okay. And who all are there at home?
Mother and brother.
Okay. So, tell me a little about why you came here?
I couldn't eat anything, I would vomit if I ate, and I would feel dizzy, and I would cough a lot sometimes.
Okay, so that's why you came to AIIMS to see a doctor. What did the doctor say?
The doctor gave some tests, and after seeing the test reports, he said I needed to be admitted. This treatment wouldn't be possible from home.
Okay. Can you tell me why these problems were happening? Like suddenly you couldn't eat, were vomiting, and feeling dizzy.
I was drinking too much.
Okay, at what age did you first start drinking?
Around 25-26.
How did you start initially?

First, there was a puja in the neighborhood, and friends said there was an event, a function, dancing and singing, and I would get bored sitting alone, so they asked me to drink with them. They said, "Drink a little with us, then if you want, you can leave or stay." So, that's how it first started.
Okay, so that's how it started. So, you hadn't drunk before that, right?
No, I hadn't.
How did you feel after drinking for the first time that day?
My head felt a little dizzy after drinking, I sat quietly, felt a little good. I didn't drink anymore that day. When I went home, my father was there, and my mother scolded and hit me a little.
Okay. Do you remember how much you drank that day?
About 60 ml.
Okay.
Then it was once a year. Then one day they called me again, they had some program.
Your neighborhood friends?

Yes. That day there were many kinds of food, and they said even if I don't drink, I should sit with them and eat the food. So that day, after some hesitation, I drank about 60 ml again. I ate whatever food was there, then came home and went to sleep.
Hmm, okay.
Then it continued like that, one or two days every six or nine months. Then when I was around 28-29, my circle of friends grew, and they would make me sit and drink in those places.
Okay.
It kept increasing like this until I reached this position.
Okay, so you started around 25 years old, and then it increased to 28-29 years old. Were you drinking regularly then?
I wasn't drinking regularly then; it became regular only in the last 6 months.
Okay. You used to drink with friends at various events, has it ever happened that you drank alone?
Alone, only recently.
How long ago was that?

After the first admission here, it stopped for about 1-1.5 months, then I started again. Then I saw that drinking with friends was costing too much money, so from then on, I drank alone. The quantity also increased a bit then.
Hmm. Approximately how much did you drink in a day?

I didn't drink during the day, I drank at night. Around 200 ml, sometimes 180 ml. When I went home, I couldn't sleep, so I would drink a little more, everyone had separate bedrooms. To fall asleep quickly, I would drink a little more. Sleep would come immediately.
So, how much is it in total?
Around 200-250 ml.
Okay. What was the most you had in a single day?
The most was during Durga Puja, around 250-280 ml. That happened for a day or two, on days when the program was high quality, it was from evening till all night.
Okay. How much did you spend on alcohol per month for drinking?

I didn't spend much per month; those who invited me would spend. They would say, "Drink, what's there? Today he paid, tomorrow I or someone else will." So, about 5000-5500 rupees would be spent per month.
Hmm, okay. Where did this money come from?
It came from business.
What kind of business do you have?
Furniture.
Okay. How long has the business been running?
The business is from my father's time; I am looking after it now.
Okay, okay. When you started drinking regularly or daily, let's say from 6 months ago...

I wasn't drinking regularly, but if I didn't drink, I would cough a lot, feel dizzy. I didn't vomit then, but I coughed a lot, and cough medicine didn't help. But if I drank a little, 60 or 80 ml, everything would be fine, it would become normal.
So you only drank at night, not during the day, right?
No.
What time did you sit down at night?
From around 9-9:30, it would go until 10-10:30.
Okay. You said that on days you didn't drink, you felt dizzy and coughed, and drinking made it better.
Yes. It would gradually get better.
Were there any other problems if you didn't drink?
No.
When was the last time you drank?
The last time was 3-4 days before being admitted here, on December 31st.
Did you drink alone that day?
No, it was a picnic that day, with everyone.
Okay. Towards the end, since you were drinking a lot, did you feel like drinking as soon as you woke up in the morning?

No, I didn't feel like it on an empty stomach then. I had to be at work during the day, and I didn't want to lose weight or have the workers smell it, so I didn't drink in the morning or during the day.
Okay. So you never drank during the day except at night?
No, but if there was a picnic or puja, then maybe I drank once or twice in the afternoon.
Okay. In your family, do your parents or their siblings have any addiction problems?
I heard my father did.
What was your father addicted to?
Alcohol.
Okay. Besides alcohol, what other addiction do you have?
I used to smoke 2-3 cigarettes a day.
Okay. When did you first start smoking?
That started after alcohol.
What was the most cigarettes you had in a day?
Around 4-5 during Puja, and normally 2-3 when I was at work.
Okay, so 4-5 at events and 2-3 on normal days.
Yes.
And did you have any problems if you didn't smoke?
No, there were no problems because of that.
Okay. Any other mental health problems besides addiction?
No, no.
Do you have any physical problems?
No.
Okay, I think you've had quite a few tests here, for your liver, do you know what those reports are like?
Yes, Madam said the reports are fine, but that GGT or something is high, and it will gradually get better with medication.
Hmm. So how are you feeling now?
I'm feeling much better now. I feel relaxed.
How is your mood?
It's fine generally, but being confined here for so long feels a bit strange, it would be better if I could go outside.
Hmm. Okay, do you feel like having an addiction or alcohol now?
No, not anymore.
Okay. Do you think alcohol or smoking has given you any advantages or disadvantages?
A lot of money has been spent, and there has been a lot of physical loss, especially my liver has been very damaged.
Hmm hmm. Anything else?
And on days I drank alcohol, it was very difficult to wake up the next morning, and I felt a little less energetic.
Okay, anything else?
No more.
Do you feel like it has been beneficial in any way?

No. In the morning, I used to think, "Why did I drink last night? If I had that money, it would be mine. Why did I go to drink? I could have just come home and slept, and sleep would have come gradually."
Hmm. Did you have trouble sleeping if you didn't drink?
Drinking made me sleep better.
Okay. Did you drink alcohol at home?
I used to drink at a place near my house, then I would come home.
Was there any trouble at home because of this?
Yes, my mother would scold me whenever she found out. It was mostly with my mother; my brother would do it less.
Hmm. Okay, has it ever happened that you got very angry after drinking, and there was a fight or physical altercation?
No, that has never happened.
Okay. So now, let's say after leaving here, how confident do you feel that you can stay away from addiction?
I have thought that I will eat before the time I used to drink so that my stomach will be full. The craving for alcohol will decrease. And sleep will also come gradually.
Hmm, exactly.
I think this will be good; I will come from the shop by 9 and eat. And if there is pressure at the shop, I will buy something like cake or puffed rice from the shop, eat it, and go home.
Hmm hmm. Now do you feel you can do it?

Yes, last time it was stopped for a month and a half. But then the festivals came, and taking a little for one or two days made it regular. During Durga Puja and Kali Puja, I couldn't control it. Once I started, I couldn't control it.
Okay. Has it happened before that you tried to quit drinking? Like it was stopped for a month and a half?
When I first started, I used to drink once every 6 months or two or three times a year.
And when the quantity increased, did you try to quit?
Yes, then if someone asked if I would drink, I would say no, I won't. I would come home.
Okay. How were you during that month and a half when you didn't drink continuously?
It was good then. In the morning, around 6:30-7, I would wake up due to hunger, and my sleep was also fine.
Okay. Then it increased from the time of Puja.
Hmm hmm.
So now you are confident that you can stay away from drinking.
Yes, last time I could stay away for a month and a half, so this time I can do even more.

4D discussed:

Okay, besides this, there are some techniques, which are called 4D in short. That means some methods that start with 4 D's. So the first D is distraction, which means keeping your mind busy with something else. When you feel like indulging, you do some other work. Or you talk to someone else. Or you do something you like, or watch a favorite video. Then the next D is deep breathing, meaning when you feel like indulging, a restlessness is created, right? So then we can take deep breaths. We also call this box breathing, where we can inhale through the nose for 4 seconds, hold for 4 seconds, then exhale through the mouth for 4 seconds, then hold again for 4 seconds and inhale again. We can do this 10 times. Then you will feel a little relaxed, and the restlessness will decrease, and the craving will also decrease. Three is Delay. Delaying this desire you are having.
Meaning, delay as much as possible.

Yes, convincing yourself like, not now, a little later, or after two hours, and then if the desire comes again after two hours, delaying yourself again. In this way, delaying yourself as much as possible. When you see that you have been able to stay for one or two days by delaying, then you yourself will get more encouragement from within to quit. And the 4th one is drinking water. For example, when you feel the urge, if you drink some cold water, the desire will lessen a bit. You can do these along with the methods you are currently trying. That will reduce your desire at least a little. It's better to eat less sweet things, they can have other effects on the body.

Just like when learning to ride a bicycle, if someone holds the back, it's easier to learn, similarly, nicotine gum will make quitting smoking easier for you. And there's a method to eating nicotine gum. You don't chew it too much, you chew it gently and then keep it between your cheek and gum. It will slowly spread throughout your mouth. After some time, you can move it from this cheek to that cheek. But don't chew it too much. If you keep it for half an hour, it's enough, then you can throw it away and take another one after two hours. And you should take it from the morning. Otherwise, if you take gum when you feel like smoking a cigarette, it will be difficult to stop. Because if you feel like eating biryani and you have to eat dal-bhat there, you won't like it, right?
Yes.
Hmm, try it this way once, if that doesn't work, then we can try something else. We can also try keeping something else in our mouth or trying to stay away from where others are smoking, if possible.
Okay, I'll try again.
How is your sleep and appetite now?
My sleep is not very good now. Once I wake up, it's difficult to fall asleep again.
What kind of thoughts come to your mind when you wake up?
Different thoughts come at different times. Nothing good comes. Mostly I worry about the boys.

I understand that the situation you are going through is having a big impact on your daily life, and what happened was not in your control, right? It happened suddenly, right? Accidents can happen at any time even if we don't want them to because we can't control them. Okay.

I am not well because of this bidi and leg pain, you see. I am trying to quit bidi and fix this pain.

I am 60 years old now.

Say, since I was 20 years old.

No, I haven't had any addiction other than bidis and tea. I don't go near alcohol consumption at events, or other such things.

It's been about 20 years. Two-three packets have been going on for about 10-15 years.
Okay.
And tea. I won't lie. I drink a lot of tea.

Tea is always there, and when I'm worried, it's these bidis and tea that cause me trouble. Worries about family, income, expenses, all these thoughts.

Yes, these things almost always cause tension. Financial tension, sons, worries about the house, income is not that good. Earlier, 30 kg of milk was sold in my tea shop, now not even 5 kg of milk goes to the tea shop. My income is gone. So I worry.

900 rupees a month if I consider 30 rupees daily.

I have reduced them to 10 rupees now. I smoke 1 packet. I started doing this since I started coming here. I came here because of the pain of the disease.

It must be 7 months. I want to make arrangements so that I can get rid of it completely.
How long after waking up do you have to smoke a cigarette or bidi? 5 minutes / 30 minutes / 1 hour / or more?

When I wake up in the morning, I feel like smoking a bidi. I've been trying for the last few days and I'm doing well. It feels like 5 minutes or immediately after waking up. And I need tea as soon as I wake up. I'm telling my daughters-in-law now not to give me tea. I wake up at 5, so I'm telling them to give me tea at 8.

No, whenever I eat something, like drinking tea or eating something, my craving increases.

I smoke in the morning too. I run a shop all day. So I smoke casually in the shop too.

Regarding my addiction... I wish to be free from addiction.

Now I'm old, so many diseases are increasing, mental pain is increasing, I can't walk, children, doctors, everyone is saying that my breathing problem will not be cured unless I quit bidis. My medicine is useless. So now I want to quit this.

I feel like I won't be able to do it from my heart until I get a bad taste in my mouth or something. It won't happen for me. It's not leaving my mind. I just felt like all my attention was on that. If that wasn't there, maybe I could have quit.

Hmm, this feeling of irritation, not liking it, or constantly feeling like you want to smoke, we call that withdrawal. After many years of smoking, the body develops a habit, and whenever the addiction stops or the amount decreases, various symptoms appear in the body.

It's not like I *have* to smoke a cigarette, it just feels like my brain will relax a bit. Like tea and coffee cause stimulation, a cigarette is like stimulation for me. But after having tea or coffee, I need another cigarette.
From what you're saying, it seems you want to quit smoking completely. And you're trying a lot for that. But somewhere, withdrawal symptoms are causing you trouble. Do you smoke alone or with others?

We call these withdrawal symptoms. What's happening in your case is that you've been smoking for almost 43 years, right? So your brain and body know that you'll get a bidi or cigarette in the morning and one in the evening. Now, when you don't give it that, an irritation is created, a craving is created. This is a harmful aspect of addiction, that your body and mind are dependent on such a chemical. And you also said that it's affecting your body. The carbon monoxide in bidis or cigarettes, which shouldn't be in our bodies, is entering our bodies due to smoking, right? We need to think about these things a bit. There's something called nicotine gum, like chewing gum. That will help reduce your urge to smoke twice a day.

When you are tense and want to smoke, we can use some methods. We call this the 4D technique. The first D is deep breathing. What do we do in that? When you want to smoke a cigarette, take a deep breath in and out. The next D is delay, meaning delaying smoking. Tell yourself, "Not now, I'll smoke an hour later," and then again, "Not now, another hour later." This way, you'll see a lot of time will pass without smoking.

Maybe you watched something on your phone, listened to music, or talked to a close friend, whatever you want to do. The fourth D is drinking water, meaning drinking cold water when you want to smoke. Because of this trembling neck.
Do you think there's any connection between addiction and your neck trembling?
I don't know. I'll understand if I quit. If it reduces, I won't consume it anymore.
Are you facing any other problems due to addiction? Or do you know of any other problems that can arise from smoking?
This neck.
Okay, what are the good aspects of smoking?
I just smoke.
There must be something you like about it, right? Otherwise, you wouldn't smoke 7-8 a day, right?
It feels good. Nothing else. It's become a habit.
When does your cigarette consumption increase?
I haven't smoked because I'm here now. Otherwise, if I were outside with friends, I would have smoked by now. It increases when I'm tense.
Now I will ask some questions. How long after waking up in the morning do you have a bidi? 5 min/30 min/60 min/or longer?
I wake up and go to study, then I come back and smoke with friends. Around 10 o'clock.
So how long after waking up is it?
It would be 3-4 hours.
Does it happen that you can stop yourself in places where smoking is prohibited?
No, no, it's not difficult.
For example, if you go to school or come to this hospital, is it difficult here?
No, it doesn't happen like that. I'm here now, and nothing is happening.
Which bidi would be most difficult for you to quit? The one you smoke first thing in the morning or the ones you smoke later in the day?
It would be difficult if I don't smoke throughout the day.
Okay, do you smoke more during the day or later?
I smoke more at night.
Has it ever happened that you were so sick that you couldn't get out of bed, but you still had to smoke a bidi?
No, that doesn't happen. I don't smoke at home. I smoke with the boys in the neighborhood.
What do your family members say about you smoking with the boys in the neighborhood?
They tell me not to smoke. They tell me not to because I'm young.
What do you think is the reason they tell you not to?
Smoking bidis causes cancer. It harms the heart. It harms the lungs.
Okay, you know that these kinds of harms occur, but it's still difficult for you to quit, right? What could be the reason for that?
I don't know.
Okay, for example, when friends come and offer you to smoke, can you refuse?
If they offer, I smoke then. Sometimes I say I'll smoke a little later.
Hmm, now can we think a bit about what feels good about smoking?
I don't really know that. If I have a bidi, I smoke one.
But there must be some good aspect to smoking, right? Since you know it's harmful, but you can't quit. Can you think about it at home so we can discuss it next time?
Next time?
Yes, you don't have to say it now. Next time.
I can't think of anything right now.

Hmm, okay, for example, chocolate, many people like it, right? They like it because something about it feels good. Maybe someone likes the taste of chocolate, right? In your case, what do you like about bidis?
The taste is not my reason. But I can't really say.
Okay, then we'll talk about this next time. Now tell me, you said your smoking increases when you're tense, right? What are the reasons your tension increases?
Sitting here, I feel tense. When I go to study and the sir gives me something to write, and I can't do it, then I get tense. Sometimes my mother argues, then I get tense.
Okay, what happens when you're tense? Do your hands shake?
No, then my neck trembles.
Is there anything that stops your neck from trembling?
It gets better when I lie down.
Do the people around you notice that your neck is trembling? I mean, is it visible from the outside?
Yes, they do.

When you feel very tense, you can try this method. For example, if you're studying and your neck is trembling, sit quietly and take a deep breath for 4 seconds, then hold it for 4 seconds, then slowly exhale, and then hold it for 4 seconds again and take another breath. We will do this 10 times when you feel tense. We can do this, right? You will feel very relaxed. Then your neck trembling will also reduce, right? Try it at home and see if it helps.
Yes, I will try it.
How are you?
Not very well.
Hmm, who all are there at home?
My wife and I live in our Durgapur house. And my parents live 25 km away from there.
Okay, what do you do?
I work in IT. I work from home.
Okay, why did you have to come here today?

Here, we are family planning and my wife has PCOD. Okay? So when PCOD treatment is going on, semen analysis needs to be done. Then suddenly it was found that the semen count is very low. Dangerously low. Motility rate is 3%. Then the doctor there asked if I smoke. So I said yes, I do. So for that, they referred me to a urologist and a psychiatrist.
What is the urologist saying?
I have an appointment in urology. It hasn't happened yet. It will be post-lunch.
Okay, you said you smoke, right? From what age did you start?
From 2007.
So how old were you then?
12-13 years old. The chain started after 2016.
When it first started, how did it start?
It started with friends. Like bad company, you know. Friends teach bad things too. They teach good things too.
Do you only smoke cigarettes?
Yes. Beedi is not smoked much. During lockdown, people smoked everything due to scarcity.
Okay, anything else like khaini or paan?
No.
Roughly how many cigarettes do you smoke a day?

Yes, I'll tell you. When I first came, it was a month ago. Not even a month. In the middle of last month, I used to smoke 25 to 30 a day. Then when I found out I had to reduce, now it's 12-15 a day for me. If I try to reduce more than that, a terrible craving comes where I can't stay, like after food, after tea. If I don't drink tea, I think I won't drink tea, then maybe there won't be a craving, but that's not happening either. Then I get a headache. It has become a habit. So if I don't drink tea, I get a headache.
How did it increase so much? 25 to 30?
I used to smoke even more than that. I used to smoke almost 50.
Hmm hmm.
That was 2017-2018. Then during the lockdown, it reduced to 25 to 30. Then recently, I reduced this much in a month.
Okay. So how much money are you spending on smoking per month now?
All kinds of things have happened. If I talk about last month. The smoking bill was 9500 rupees. This month that bill is 3434 rupees.
Okay, so you are keeping track?

It's done monthly. Wherever I go, it's done monthly. The calculation stays there, otherwise I don't keep track. Honestly, I wouldn't keep track. It's been two months since I started planning, since then I decided to keep track of how much money is being spent. So it has reduced now. This month the target is to bring it down to 1000 or 2000. I mean, I'm trying my best... but sometimes when that craving comes or I fall into bad company, the problem is there, isn't it? Maybe he's smoking, then I say, "Give me a puff." From that, the count increases, the puffs increase. It takes me two-three days to reduce that again. I might have struggled to bring it down to 12 or 10, then suddenly something happened...
Okay, in what situations does the craving increase or occur?

One is when I'm working, meaning I'm at home and it's available, I know I can smoke, then it happens. Speaking of today, I entered here at 8 o'clock. It's been so long since 8 o'clock, I haven't smoked a single cigarette yet.
Are you craving now?

I haven't craved because psychologically it's running in my mind that I can't smoke here. Until I can get out of the hospital, I can't smoke. Then I am a Brahmin, I do puja, housewarming, weddings, it seems my whole day passes. There is no craving. As long as I am sitting on the puja mat, no craving. The moment I get up from the puja mat, I feel like one would be good. It's like that. The moment I smoke one, that's it. I'm talking about normal days, I wake up, sit on the bed, drink water, and then a cigarette. Without that, my journey to the prayer room doesn't happen. I'll go to the prayer room, then finish all the morning chores, sit down, then have tea and a cigarette. I'll have breakfast, a cigarette. If I eat heavy food, one is not enough if I'm subconsciously smoking. Eating has to be consciously. Maybe I'm on my phone or working, then it's between my fingers...
Hmm hmm.

While it's between my fingers, see, this spot has appeared. This spot is due to nicotine. Now it's in my hand, I might be working, maybe I haven't smoked. Maybe I've only taken 3-4 puffs of a whole cigarette. But it's finished. Maybe I've been riding a bike for a long time, holding it in my hand, on the bike, it finishes with two puffs at that speed. So holding it in the finger is also a habit. Plus, it's something that has to be smoked timely. Now I'm working, while working on the laptop, the cigarette is between my fingers and I subconsciously smoked it, so I didn't feel like I smoked. That's why I have to consciously smoke another one.
Hmm, I understand.

Now I'm trying to reduce that, that whenever I smoke a cigarette, I will focus on the cigarette. I will feel every puff. I'm trying to make that a habit. It's a long-standing habit, so it's taking some time.
Okay, do you think this has helped in any way?

By doing that, by being conscious, I have been able to reduce it this much so far. What's happening is that since it's a habit to hold it in my fingers when I work in my subconscious mind, I don't keep cigarettes with me. I keep them in the car downstairs and I live on the 4th floor. Maybe I take two up, and when they're finished, I have to go down again. There will be a laziness in the body then, and I won't feel like going down. If laziness takes over, I don't smoke, and if craving takes over, I'll go down and smoke.
I can see that you are trying hard to reduce it.

I'm trying my best. It's been 30 years and my wife is 28. She has PCOD, and if we have such problems, then conceiving will be a problem. And you know what happens in villages, after 5 years of marriage, people start talking. And all the blame is put on the wife. That's why I have to do it.
Hmm, has it ever happened before that you tried to quit?
When I smoked in 2007, I used to smoke. But from mid-2011 to 2013, I studied in a residential campus, then I wouldn't smoke for months, maybe two-three months straight, and there was no problem then.
And you said chain smoking didn't happen...
No, not after chain smoking.
If you are asked to quit smoking, which time's cigarette would be most difficult for you to quit? The one in the morning or the ones during the rest of the day?

The one I smoke after eating food. After eating at any time. I'm trying not to bring it from downstairs in the morning. If I bring it at night, I finish it by night and then don't bring it again. No matter how much I crave, I can only bring it in the morning after all the chores are done and I go downstairs. So there's a gap in the morning. But for example, after breakfast in the morning or lunch or tea, it's a must. It feels like tea isn't complete without it.
I understand. Okay, do you have to smoke more during the day or later?
It starts in the morning and continues till night. I stay at home, teach tuition, do puja, I smoke during everything.
Do you think it would be easier if you didn't work from home and went to the office?
No, then I'll get stuck in something else. My office is in Ahmedabad.
Hmm, I understand. Has it ever happened that you were very sick, bedridden, and still had to smoke a cigarette?

Gallbladder stone operation happened in 2016, okay? It happened in Anandlok. It happened at 7 in the evening, and there's a hangover from anesthesia. I went in with cigarettes, I caught the ward boy, I told him, "Brother, you take one, I'll take one." With that pain, he took me to the bathroom all night, and I smoked 10 cigarettes all night, and in the morning I was OK. I mean, the craving is at such a level that when I'm in pain, a cigarette is the only thing that can help me.
I understand. Besides that, do you have any other addiction?
Alcohol occasionally, but very limited. Maybe one peg or if someone insists a lot, two pegs, no more than that.
When did you last drink?
January 1st.
Okay, ever ganja?
Tried it once in my life.
Okay, any physical problems?
Diabetes is borderline. It was detected after coming here. My blood pressure is normal as far as I know. I have obesity. I have migraines.
Okay, how is your mood generally?
I am not quarrelsome. Where there is conflict, I am not there. I don't know what God's will is, I just get involved there. I like to be alone. I stay busy with work.
Is anyone else at home addicted?

My grandfather was. But if I had my grandfather's quitting ability, it would have been even better. I don't have that. He used to smoke 4 bundles of beedi a day. Then he quit. From 4 bundles to zero. That's not possible for me. I've tried to quit three times for this. I've used chewing gum, Ayurvedic tablets, patches, nothing is working.
How long did you use the patch?

I used it for a month, but then I didn't have the consistency or the desire to quit that much. People were forcing me, that's why. But now I see I have to quit, there's no other way. Those two bottles of Ayurvedic medicine are still at home, and even without taking them, I've been able to bring it down from 25-30 to 12-15 directly.
How do you feel about being able to reduce it so much?

When you asked me to think about it, there's a war going on in my mind that it would have been good to smoke one. It's only been a month since I reduced it, so I'm not even getting the scope to think about it. I am in war all the time. I always have to think, not now, I'll smoke later.
Okay, how do you feel about wanting to conceive but it's not happening?
There's a little tension.
Is there any relation between that and smoking, like craving increasing or something?

These small tensions or office tensions don't have an impact. As boys, we've always been taught to be strong, to handle these things. You could say that this has become more of a habit than an addiction. How do I quit this habit?
Now that you're trying so hard to quit, one reason is health, what other reasons are there?
If I think about it now, I have many reasons to hold on. But there's only one reason to quit.
Okay, what are the reasons to hold on?

I like to smoke. Smoking feels different. A group has formed. I have a group of smokers. There are many things that provoke me to smoke. Another reason could be that my wife will be happy. There's nothing else. I practice Tantra, so smoking and drinking are part of it.
For example, if we have a child, could smoking have any effect on that?

Of course, if I quit, they can live in a good place. I smoke with the windows closed in the car wherever I go, that will affect the newborn. Passive smoking happens. If I can quit, there are many benefits to that.
So you only have one reason to quit, right, that you are family planning?
Yes.
It's very good that you yourself are trying many methods to quit the addiction. Can anything else be tried along with that?
Like what?

Um, this is the 4D technique. The first D is that you know you're craving and you know you can't go downstairs, so you delay going downstairs, saying I won't go now, I'll go after 30 minutes, then after some time, I'll smoke after 1 hour, by doing this, you keep pushing it back.
My wife also says this. When I crave, it's very difficult for me to do this.
Okay, so what else can we do?

I'm doing one thing, I'm buying less. Now I'm buying 12. I have to walk or bike 900 meters to go to the market from home. Now I feel lazy to go that far and bring it. Then I don't smoke. But yes, if you say you'll put me in rehab, even if you keep me there for a month, I won't smoke, but the day I go out, it will start again.
So availability is a reason why you are having trouble winning.
Yes, that's the biggest reason. I'm slowly trying to reduce having it with me.
So can we try buying a little less than 12?
10. I'm buying one packet from tomorrow. It's still going, 8 are left, I hope it will last today.
How does it make you feel that you've been able to reduce it so much, that now 10 cigarettes last you two days?
Good. I see now that if I smoke consciously, what used to take one cigarette now takes two puffs. If I crave a lot, I take two puffs and then leave it.

Okay, that's also a good method. From now on, nicotine gum will be given, which will help you speed up or make this quitting process a little easier. Nicotine gum will help reduce this craving or desire to smoke.
Yes, if my craving is controlled, I can quit completely.
How are you?
I'm not doing very well. I don't know if this will require surgery or if medicine will cure it.
What problem are you having?
It's written here, I don't understand much.
Hmm, urinary problems, right?
Hmm, hmm, bleeding.
Shortness of breath is also written.
Yes, yes. Will these things not be cured with medicine without surgery?
What is the doctor saying?
The doctor said to see him after showing these departments. An USG needs to be done.
Okay. How long have you had this illness?

It's been a long time. 3-4 years. I didn't give it much importance then. How do I say it? I went to Telangana by car. I couldn't eat there. All their food was sour. Sour food doesn't taste good in winter, right? I couldn't eat and couldn't return the same day. The next day I went again with goods. Then in the morning when I went to the bathroom, I saw blood in my urine. I thought my body might have gotten hot, that's why it's like this. But it didn't decrease. Then I saw a local doctor first, and he said I needed surgery. Then I asked if it wouldn't be possible without surgery? Then I saw someone at Dum Dum Sithi More, and he also suggested surgery. After that, he gave me some medicine. If I took it, blood would clot and block the urinary tract. In the meantime, my wife suddenly died. I mean, a healthy person. She cooked on Monday, and couldn't cook on Tuesday. Her head was heavy, she was just sleeping.
Oh, when did this happen?

It's been about a month and a half. She died on May 23rd. When the disease was diagnosed, it was found to be a brain stroke. A lot of water came out of her nose. She took medicine at noon. She was also given medicine in the evening. But at 7 in the evening, she was no longer there, she passed away. I didn't get to see her. I was sitting in front of the gate. Anyway, what can be done now? Seeing the doctor was slowly postponed. I didn't go again. Then I came to AIIMS. Everyone says AIIMS is very good. I've come three days in a row for this. I'll do all the blood tests, chest exams, ECG, echo, and so on, that they've given me dates for. The cost is low. For now, I'm not working, I'm just sitting. People work. I don't go anywhere from home. My legs hurt if I walk too much. I don't know why. I haven't had any alcohol or anything like that. I'm in the driving line, but I've never had it. I smoke a couple of bidis.
Okay, are you living alone now?

No, we all live together. My work all day is drinking water. I have an elder sister. My granddaughter. She stays home all day Saturday, doesn't go to school. Her mother is a school teacher. Her father does some writing work at a bus depot in New Town. That's our family. Before this, she used to live with her grandmother. Now she doesn't go anywhere. Everyone is saying to sell it. That's what I'm thinking about now. I'll slowly let go now.
Now you're also at retirement age, right?

Yes, I am. I'm 66-67. My eldest sister-in-law is alive, all my sisters-in-law are alive. My father passed away, my mother passed away, my elder brother and younger brother all passed away, but my aunt is still alive. She still does her own work. She walks with a stick. This is our family.
How much is the passing of so many people affecting your mind?

It affects me a lot, very much. It was over 40, maybe 42 or 45 years ago. That's when I got married, it's not a recent thing. It's been a long time, over 40 years. Our family is like this, there's never been any conflict. My son, daughter-in-law, everyone is very good, I have a granddaughter. Very good. If someone buys something, no one ever asks for money. We were very peaceful. Her passing is the only conflict. The mental pressure is high now because I stay home a lot, so I feel very sad. It's very painful. What more can I say?
Do you ever feel like it would be better to die or that you don't want to live?
Yes, I often feel that way. I wonder if I will die, if I can handle the surgery.
Okay, how are your sleep and appetite?

Sleep... sometimes I don't sleep all night. Other times, I fall asleep after 10 for a couple of days. I have to go to the bathroom 7-8 times. Sleep is difficult. And after she passed away, my eating is also difficult. She used to cook very well.
When you can't sleep, do you worry a lot?
No, not really. I don't know why I can't sleep.
Any other physical problems?
No, nothing else. The pain of someone leaving cannot be explained. She was very beautiful. Very beautiful and tall.
Okay, I understand. There's a sense of loneliness, right?
Yes, very much.
Okay, you said you have to smoke bidis, right? Can you say how old you were when you started?

Yes, I can. I was studying then. I studied up to class 4. Then my father died from a snake bite. We are farmers. Our home is in a village. We were three brothers. Then we had to start farming. That's how it went.
Hmm, okay. How old were you when you were in class 4?
I was 16-17 years old then.
How many bidis do you smoke a day?
I smoke 8-10 a day. They have forbidden it, saying not to smoke anymore.
Hmm, so what are you thinking?

If I want to live, I have to stop. I chew a couple of ajwain seeds. I smoke fewer bidis now. Now I smoke 4, 5, 3 like that. My son gets very angry. My granddaughter breaks them. I have a box of ajwain. It lasts two or three days. Everyone says there's no problem with ajwain. I eat a little. Maybe I put a couple in my mouth after eating rice.
Do you chew khaini?
No, I've never chewed khaini, alcohol, or any other addiction in my life. I never got the chance. I have 7 siblings. It was very difficult to raise them and educate them.
Oh, how long after waking up in the morning do you have your first bidi? 5 minutes / 30 minutes / 1 hour / or more time later?
I smoke a bidi when I go to the bathroom in the morning after waking up. That would be about half an hour.
Is it difficult to stop yourself in places where smoking is forbidden?
No, not at all. It feels strange if I don't smoke a bidi. My mouth feels weird. I don't even drink tea.
Hmm, which bidi would be the hardest for you to give up - the one you smoke first thing in the morning or the ones later in the day?
I can tolerate all the others.
But still, which one would be the hardest?
The one in the morning when I go to the bathroom, it's convenient to smoke that one. That one.
Hmm, do you smoke more in the morning or later in the day?
I smoke bidis all day. It's very difficult to say. Sometimes when I'm stressed, I smoke two or three. Other days, I might not smoke all day.
Has your smoking increased after your wife passed away?
No, no. It has even decreased.
Has it ever happened that you were so sick that you were confined to bed, but you still had to smoke a bidi?
Yes, it happens. Although I haven't been so sick that I couldn't get out of bed. My deadly disease is this bleeding.
Have you ever been able to quit smoking for many days at a stretch?
I've never done that. But I've quit for 2-5 days. I just didn't smoke. I didn't feel like it, and they weren't available far away, so I didn't smoke.
You never quit with the intention of quitting, right?
No, no.
Does anyone else in your house have an addiction?
No. No one else in our house smokes.
Does anyone have a major physical or mental illness?
No, everyone in the house is healthy.
Okay, now about your addiction?
I will quit bidis.
Hmm, but why do you want to quit?
For my own health. The doctors also want me to quit. For these reasons, I won't smoke.
It says here that you have a cough. How long have you had this cough?
I've had this cough for a long time. It's been 4-5 years, a very bad cough. A cough where I can't breathe. I saw a doctor then. He gave me an inhaler then. I still use it occasionally.
Okay, is there any connection between your bidi/cigarette addiction and your shortness of breath?

I don't know, I don't know much. But everyone says smoking bidis causes coughing. There are also many pictures and writings on bidi packets. These things come to mind. I am a very strong person, but after this, I have become soft.
Has chewing ajwain reduced your desire to smoke bidis?
It has reduced a lot. After eating rice, I put two or four in my mouth, and that's it.
You were saying that you feel lonely. Is there anyone around you with whom you can talk?
Yes, I feel lonely. I stay alone at home. Everyone calls, but no one comes home. I talk on the phone. I don't even go to the market.

Okay, okay. It says here about nicotine gum. It will help reduce your desire to smoke bidis. It reduces your craving for bidis. How do you take it? You chew it a little and keep it between your cheek and gum. It will greatly reduce your desire to smoke bidis. This is a habit of so many years, so it will be a little difficult to quit, and nicotine gum will make it a little easier. You can keep it for 30-45 minutes and then discard it. Then you have to take it again after 2-3 hours.
Ajwain also works. But if you tell me to take this, I'll take this.
Okay.
How are you?
I am fine.
Okay, why did you have to come here?
For addiction.
What kind of addiction do you have?
Bidi and cigarettes. Not much. Bidi is stopped, cigarettes are smoked occasionally.
At what age did it start?
It's been a long time. From about 18-19 years old. Now 65.
Okay, how many bidis or cigarettes do you smoke in a day?
Now 3-4 cigarettes a day.
How many did you use to smoke before?
Before I used to smoke a lot. About 10-11 years ago, I used to smoke about 2 packets.
Hmm, meaning 20, right? How long did you smoke like that?
For that long. I stopped for 15 days. But it started again recently. I will stop again from today.
Hmm, and khaini?
No, nothing like that.
Okay, now I will ask you some questions about addiction. How long after waking up do you have a bidi/cigarette? 5 min/30 min/1 hour or more time later?
Much more than an hour. For example, if I wake up at 6 AM, I have the first one around 8:30 AM.
Do you have difficulty stopping yourself in places where smoking is prohibited, such as schools or hospitals?
No. Not at all.
Which cigarette is the hardest to quit, the one you smoke first in the morning or the ones you smoke during the rest of the day?
None of them are hard to quit. The problem arises when friends gather to play cards. I don't smoke cigarettes/bidis when I'm at home. It only happens when I go out.
When do you play cards with your friends?

Around 4 o'clock. Before that, I have one or two between one and two. Then I have about four between 4 and 7. After that, it's completely stopped again. I don't smoke when I go home at night, and I don't smoke after eating.
And when you used to smoke more?
Before, it wasn't measured. Whenever I wanted, all the time.
Has it ever happened that you were very sick, but you still had to smoke a cigarette or bidi?
No. Never happened.
How much money do you spend on your addiction?
I've quit now. Before, it used to cost maybe 20-30 rupees daily. So, about 300-400 rupees a month.
Has it ever happened that you were able to quit addiction for a long time?
Yes, it was completely stopped for two years. It started again now.
Why did you have to stop?
Because of stomach problems. I had stomach problems, had to take medicine, and I thought I wouldn't eat anymore, and I didn't.
Two years is a long time\! Why did it start again now?
Friends forced me, I had one or two, and then it started. I will quit again from today.
Do you have any other addictions besides this?
No. Occasionally with friends. It happens every two months or so. It's been 20-22 days since I last drank alcohol.
When you drink, how much do you drink?
About 175 ml.
Anything else?
No, never.
Do you have any physical or mental problems?
My sugar is increasing a bit. The report came today.
Is there any connection between your stomach problems and your smoking?
No, I don't understand that. Suddenly, after eating, my stomach feels heavy. That's all.
How is everything at home?
Everything is fine. I have two sons, their wives, and my wife.
How is your mood?
My mood is fine. It's good. Of course, if there's no money, there will be some worry.
Okay, when you have this money worry, does your addiction increase or anything like that?
No, it doesn't happen like that.
Does anyone else at home have an addiction?
No, no one.
Okay, I see that you have reduced your addiction a lot, right? What is the reason for that?

I don't like it. I'm old now. I have to see so many doctors, do this and that. And I'm not doing very well by eating. One day, maybe I get influenced by someone and eat too much. Then I have stomach problems. If my bowel movements are not clear, there is a problem. Otherwise, everything is fine.

Hmm, I understand, so you want to quit for this reason. Is it causing you any difficulty that your addiction is decreasing compared to before? Is there any problem that you used to smoke 20 bidis and now you are smoking 3-4?
No, it's not causing any difficulty.
Hmm, this stomach problem was there before, right? But then you couldn't reduce it, but what is the reason that it is reducing now?
I went to Chennai. I was fine after taking medicine from there. When I started addiction again, this problem increased again. I won't eat now.
You said that friends force you when you play cards. What will you do then?
I won't eat then. Let them force me, I will say that it causes problems for my body, and I won't eat anymore.
It's very good that you are trying to quit, it has reduced a lot compared to before...
Yes, it's all up to me. If I decide not to eat, I won't eat, and I won't even desire it. I will quit from my heart.
Hmm, okay.
How are you?

Harm to the body, meaning?

Smoking khaini and cigarettes affects semen count.

Okay, there's a nicotine gum, like chewing gum. You don't chew it, you chew it once and then press it between your cheek and gum, and a juice will come out that will spread throughout your mouth. There shouldn't be.

I came here... I mean... ever since I gained consciousness, I've had one problem: acidity. Back then, I didn't smoke bidis or cigarettes, but I would get acidity after eating. I used to play sports, maybe. I didn't need medicine to reduce it; it would go away on its own. Then I developed a problem with bowel movements. I used to play a lot of sports. Otherwise, there are no other problems.

The sleep problem and acidity problem are long-standing. I think I've had this problem since I was born. If I take one gas tablet a day, I'm fine all day. Whatever oily and spicy food I eat, nothing happens. But this sleep problem is recent. I used to work in Bongaon traffic. Then I was posted to the police station. My duty has been evening and night for about a year and a half. Before this, I used to sleep a lot. It even happened that I would eat lunch, sleep, and wake up the next morning. I had that much sleep. But after doing night shifts for the last year and a half, to stay awake, I would look at my phone, smoke bidis, chat, and drink tea at night. Doing all this, I don't get sleep anymore. I don't get sleep before 5 or 5:30 in the morning. Now I have morning duty. I come home again. But normal sleep is not happening anymore. It's happening every day. I'm not getting sleep. And since I'm not getting sleep, I feel like smoking a bidi or cigarette.
When you can't sleep at night, what thoughts come to your mind?

No, no thoughts. Only thoughts about sleep come. My wife and child are sleeping next to me, and I wonder why I can't sleep. This tension is there. Even when I sleep at 5 or 5:30, it's not a deep sleep. I can hear everything happening around me.
Okay, so your sleep isn't that deep, right?
Yes, exactly. Then I wake up around 9, and my head feels heavy.

Yes, Madam, I can reduce it if I want to. I smoke more bidis; cigarettes are very rare.

No, nothing else. No khaini. Maybe I drink alcohol at a friend's birthday, or not even then. If I drink alcohol, I get acidity even if I take a gas tablet. It's not like I have to drink. I avoid it as much as I can. If forced, I have one peg.

Roughly 14-15 years old.

It happens within 5-6 minutes.

That's very difficult to say. If you tell me now, I can quit right now.

The ones throughout the day.

There's only one tension. About my office. I'm not very good at English. I've now come to Bongaon PD, where everything is on the computer and in English. So, in that place, there are 7-8 officers. I go at 10 or 10:30 in the morning and stay until 8 in the evening. During that time, I feel a lot of tension about what people will say. If I make a small mistake, what will this person say, what will that person say, what are people thinking? Like, what happens is, say this is a file, I can't read what's written. My sir might show me on the computer and tell me to bring this file. Then I think, how will I find it? There are 500 files there, and I keep searching and searching for that file. Sir told me to copy the format from the file, and then I can't even say that I don't know English.

I know very little. I studied up to secondary school, but I'm very weak in English. English just doesn't enter my head. That's why I can't even say that I won't be able to write. Then I get stressed.

Yes, I can write by looking. But when they use official language, I can't do it anymore. I can't ask again. My two other friends who are working have learned by watching. Then they also say, why won't he do it? We also didn't know computers, he can use a mobile, so why can't he do this? They say things like this. This happens every day.

Try to sit in a chair and go to bed only when you feel sleepy. Looking at your phone before sleeping also makes it difficult to fall asleep. So try not to look at your phone too much before going to sleep. Lying down, you can try deep breathing. When you're not getting sleep, you can take long breaths in and out. And try to focus completely on your breathing then.

Okay, this method is a little wrong here, okay? There is a method to take nicotine gum. It is that even though it's like chewing gum, we don't eat it that way. That's why you probably didn't like the taste. So how to eat it - you take a gum, and place it between your cheek and gum. Now you have to press it gently, the juice will come out very slowly.
Okay.

And you have to keep it in your mouth for a long time. For example, you keep it on one side of your mouth and occasionally press it gently, you don't need to chew or suck it, just press it gently occasionally. Now, for example, after keeping it for a long time, your cheek feels tingly, then you move it to the other side. Do this for about forty-five minutes.
